content.plain_english: This week your child is meeting solid shapes (3-D — like balls and boxes) and flat shapes (2-D — like circles and squares). They are sorting them, naming them, and starting to spot patterns made from shapes.
content.in_school: Children handle real shapes: a ball, a tube, a cube of cheese, a triangular piece of toast. They sort them by what they look like or what they do — rolls or slides. They press flat shapes onto paper to see the outline, and put shapes in a row to make a pattern: square, circle, square, circle.
content.home_activity: On the next supermarket trip, play 'spot the shape' — find one box (cuboid), one tin (cylinder) and one orange (sphere). At home, lay out three different shapes and start a pattern: square, circle, square. Ask your child to carry it on. Three or four shapes is enough.
dinner_table_questions: Which shape on this table rolls — and which one slides?
Can you find something that is a circle?
If I make a pattern triangle-square-triangle-square, what comes next?

key_vocabulary.definition: A flat shape with length and width — like a circle or square.
A solid shape you can pick up — like a ball or a box.
The straight edge of a 2-D shape.
The point where two sides of a shape meet.
Something repeated in a regular order.

example_questions: How many corners does a triangle have?
Continue the pattern: circle, square, circle, square, ___
content.times_table_tip: Year 1 children practise counting in 2s, 5s and 10s. While naming shapes, count their sides — 3, 4, 5 — and chant: three sides, four sides, five sides.
